title: Prestonia woodsoniana
taxonomy: Prestonia woodsoniana is classified in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The Apocynaceae family is often divided into several subfamilies, with Prestonia woodsoniana belonging to the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. This subfamily includes over 2,000 species of plants, many of which are known for their unique and often ornamental flowers. The classification of Prestonia woodsoniana is further supported by its characteristics, such as its milky sap and paired leaves.

intro: Prestonia woodsoniana, also known as the 'wood's Prestonia', is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its unique succulent growth habit and attractive, funnel-shaped flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Prestonia woodsoniana include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
